objective: Quantify how hot microwave components map onto measurable qubit and cavity observables in a dispersive cQED platform, determine which observables are the best thermometers, and identify safe coherence and readout regimes in the presence of hot dissipative elements or multimode leakage.
completed_tasks: Initialized the study directory, README, IMPROVEMENTS log, and run scaffolding.
Audited the local cqed_sim API for dispersive multimode models and thermal Lindblad noise support.
Implemented the shared thermal-study simulation library and four thrust-specific workflows.
Executed thrust A-D simulations, saved machine-readable artifacts, and generated publication-quality figures.
Validated zero-temperature, Bose-scaling, truncation, low-occupation analytic, and multimode weak-coupling checks.
Wrote the markdown memo, LaTeX report, compiled PDF, and reproducibility notebook.
pending_tasks: Independent critical review and any follow-up iteration requested by the reviewer.
